title: marmaroxylon claviflorum
intro: Marmaroxylon claviflorum, also known as the "claviflorum tree" or "black ironwood," is a species of flowering plant in the family Meliaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Marmaroxylon claviflorum include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of ailments. In herbal preparations, the bark and wood of the plant can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
